75414.subject: AVALANCHE JUNE 2026 RECAP
75414.body:
View this post on the web at https://www.team1.blog/p/avalanche-june-2026-recap

June 2026 was Team1’s largest recruitment moment.
The grassroots organization opened applications globally and within days received 2,200+ submissions from 60+ countries, reaching over 3,000 applicants across 80+ countries by month’s end. This growth signals unprecedented demand for builders to join Avalanche’s community infrastructure. Simultaneously, institutional adoption accelerated: RWA transfer volume surged 11,700% in 30 days to $428.9M. The Avalanche Payments Collective launched with 28 founding members including Franklin Templeton, VanEck, and Paxos. Monthly C-Chain transactions jumped 6X compared to June 2025.
Here’s everything that went down in the month of June.
Want to keep up with the latest in the Avalanche ecosystem? Subscribe to the Team1 blog and get our updates in your inbox weekly.
RWA Transfer Volume Explodes 11,700% Over 30 Days:
Avalanche’s RWA transfer volume reached $428.9 million early in the month and kept growing as more institutions used the network. By mid-month, the amount of RWA transferred over the previous 30 days had increased by 11,700%, showing a huge rise in institutional activity. At the same time, Avalanche’s total RWA market cap grew by 12%, while distributed RWAs increased by over 22%. [RWA surge [ https://substack.com/redirect/b8ba8bae-98df-4f9e-906c-af40643d1934?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]] | [RWA growth [ https://substack.com/redirect/8ad1b25e-ba63-49f8-9d9b-ba715bfe92ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]]
C-Chain Transaction Volume Surges 6X Year-Over-Year:
Monthly transactions on AVAX C-Chain are up over 6X since June 2025, with the network processing 393.7M transactions in 2026 so far, nearly 7x higher than the same period last year.  [Transaction growth [ https://substack.com/redirect/7916decc-441c-4c7f-bd9c-6b97699f5d14?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]]
FIFA World Cup Ticketing Goes Live on Avalanche:
Right-to-Ticket redemptions for FIFA World Cup 2026 went live on FIFACollect’s Avalanche L1, with transactions surging 761% week-over-week. Over 29,000 tickets have been issued and scanned at stadiums, representing real fan activity powered by blockchain invisibly to users. [FIFA launch [ https://substack.com/redirect/0f048ae7-ce68-46d7-b4d0-d8d029d09156?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]] | [Ticket scans [ https://substack.com/redirect/03ae0505-ac3f-4c26-86ce-6c6c5cd51714?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]]
Team1 Applications Surpass 3,000 Across 80+ Countries:
Team1 received over 3,000 applications from 80+ countries within the first two weeks of opening global recruitment, with 2,200+ submissions arriving in just the first few days. Applicants self-identified as believers in blockchain technology, builders with cool projects, networke

75413.subject: 🗽 Altman's 5% sacrifice to Washington
75413.body:
----------
######                  [Collaborate with us](https://utm.genai.works/r/he2t9cz)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** OpenAI floated handing the Trump administration 5% of the company, about $43 billion of goodwill, to "clear political obstacles." Benchmarks say the Fable 5 that returned this week is a shadow of the one that left. Tesla capped employee AI spending at $200 a week, with one telling exemption. And Microsoft, days after prepping its July cuts, funded a 6,000-person army that installs AI for other companies.



**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **OpenAI:** What does 5% of OpenAI buy in Washington?

* **Claude:** Who gets more access and what changed with Fable?

* **Tesla:** Which AI survived Tesla's new $200 weekly cap?

* **Microsoft: **Why is it copying the Pentagon's favorite software company?


----------



----------
## [OpenAI offered Washington a piece of itself](https://www.ft.com/content/7c803eab-8e80-4431-9a87-e943bf00e00b?syn-25a6b1a6=1)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/0141c2f2-5133-45fd-a316-7e7fa0f95e7c/Beehiiv_01_-_Jul_3.png?t=1783071750)
Caption: 

Sam Altman's plan for getting the government out of OpenAI's way is to bring it inside.

The FT reports OpenAI has discussed giving the US government a 5% stake, about $43 billion at its $852 billion valuation, to "clear political obstacles." 

Altman suggested the number himself, in talks that included Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

The shape he floated is an Alaska-style fund. Every big American lab would put 5% of its equity into a vehicle that pays dividends to the public, the way Alaska pays residents from its oil money. 

Anthropic, Google and Meta would be expected to match, and nobody has said they will.

There's a precedent, and it worked. The government took 10% of Intel last year, and Trump went from attacking Intel's CEO to championing the company.

The talks are early, "conceptual," and might need an act of Congress. Altman has also been talking to Bernie Sanders, who wants the public share closer to 50%.

Last week the government decided who gets GPT-5.6 first. This week OpenAI offered it a seat on the cap table. 

Once the Treasury owns a slice of the company it regulates, every AI decision in Washington comes with a conflict of interest built in, and that might be exactly the point.


----------











----------
## [Anthropic caged Fable and 5x'd everything else](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/artificial-intelligence/claude-fable-5-isnt-permanently-leaving-subscriptions-anthropic-
